arts.ttl: Ruben Selles faces big Hull City decision which will impact Tigers survival bid
arts.sum: After discussions with Brighton, the 23-year-old Halifax-born stopper will remain at the MKM Stadium for the remainder of the season and will now battle it out for the number one jersey with Pandur, given Anthony Racioppi has been told he can leave the club this month. Does he stick with Ivor Pandur, or make a change and give Carl Rushworth his first Championship appearance of the season, a decision which is always viewed as a big one?

arts.ttl: Millwall new boy cites Alex Neil’s work at developing centre-backs as a lure after making switch
arts.sum: Asked about the attraction of working with new Lions head coach Alex Neil, Crama told Millwall TV : “I can name names – he had a lot of young centre-backs he developed – Sepp van den Berg, who is at Brentford, Ben Godfrey and Ben Davies. New Millwall centre-back Tristan Crama has talked about Alex Neil’s history of developing players in his position as one of the factors in joining from Brentford.

arts.ttl: Hull City's predicted starting XI for Millwall as Carl Rushworth and Joe Gelhardt decisions made
arts.sum: City's predicted starting XI: Rushworth, Drameh, McLoughlin, Jones, Coyle (c), Alzate, Slater, Puerta, Gelhardt, Kamara, Pedro. Pandur has started all 26 of City's Championship games so far, but fit-again Rushworth made his long-awaited debut in the FA Cup loss to Doncaster Rovers on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Aaron Connolly ready to down Hull City after quitting Sunderland for Millwall
arts.sum: Aaron's been a bit unlucky not to play a bit more at Sunderland this season, but that has given us an opportunity to get him in. The former City man, who departed the MKM Stadium at the end of last season after his contract wasn't renewed by the Tigers, has left Sunderland to join Saturday's opponents Millwall.

arts.ttl: Millwall confirm long-term deal for Brentford defender - and he can make his debut against Hull City
arts.sum: “We know how to develop young players, we gave them a brilliant environment and a platform to show their talent and progress their careers. “We can be really proud of that because Tristan was a player that had a fairly difficult loan last year, he didn’t play that much near the end of last season for Bristol Rovers, and here he has flourished and we have given him a platform to show his talent.

arts.ttl: Hull City expected to send ‘firm proposal’ for signing within hours
arts.sum: Hull City are expected to make a ‘firm proposal’ for Monaco midfielder Eliot Matazo in the next few hours. That’s according to L’Equipe, who report that Hull are targeting the 22-year-old and preparing a bid for him. The newspaper reports states that the Belgian midfielder could ‘bounce back’ in England despite having offers from France. The post Hull City expected to send ‘firm proposal’ for signing within hours appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Hull City signing Nordin Amrabat makes confident declaration as he prepares for Tigers debut
arts.sum: Nordin Amrabat says he's fit and ready to help Hull City's crusade to stay in the Championship after becoming the Tigers' third signing of what's becoming a busy January transfer window. The 64-cap Moroccan international, who has represented his country at the London Olympics, the World Cup in Russia and two Africa Cup of Nations, has experience of playing in the Premier League with Watford, having joined the Hornets for £6m in 2016, and will help mentor the club's younger players through what will be a challenging end to the campaign.

arts.ttl: Hull City preparing €2 million offer for Monaco’s Eliot Matazo
arts.sum: As initially reported by Sacha Tavolieri, Hull City hold an interest in AS Monaco’s Eliot Matazo (22). However, an update from L’Équipe, adds that the Championship side are preparing an…

arts.ttl: Nordin Amrabat Hull City signing explained as Ruben Selles details logic behind surprise deal
arts.sum: Selles, though, says Amrabat will provide good experience for the likes of Leeds United loanee Joe Gelhardt and Abu Kamara, while they navigate through a tough period without star summer signings Liam Millar and Mohamed Belloumi who are both sidelined with ACL injuries. After three-and-a-half years in Athens, playing a key role in helping AEK win the league and cup double, the 64-cap Moroccan international hasn't played a competitive game since November 3, but Selles says after training for the first time with City on Thursday, he looks in good shape.

arts.ttl: Blackburn Rovers and Hull City join Championship fight for Liverpool prodigy
arts.sum: Blackburn Rovers, Hull City and Oxford United are the latest sides credited with interest in Liverpool’s Jayden Danns… The post Blackburn Rovers and Hull City join Championship fight for Liverpool prodigy appeared first on The72 - Football League News.
